steps.title: Identify the Grammatical Structures
steps.content: Before the blank: incomplete appositive noun phrase (“a record label that was a pioneer of early hip hop”). After the blank: independent clause starting with the subject “Chambers.”

steps.title: Apply the Appositive Punctuation Rule
steps.content: Appositives in the middle of a sentence must be enclosed by commas on both sides to mark their boundaries. The appositive needs “music” to complete the noun phrase, and then requires a closing comma before the main clause resumes. Choice A provides this comma; the others either omit it (D), misuse a semicolon (B), or introduce an awkward relative clause (C). That matches choice A — A is correct.
